core_intuitions: KV tensors are a deterministic pure function of token IDs and fixed weights, so any byte-identical shared prefix yields identical KV that need be computed only once and reused across every request.

Chained per-block content hashing lets the scheduler find the longest cached prefix and cleanly split a prompt into a HIT prefix (skip prefill entirely) and a MISS suffix (run prefill, then insert its blocks into the cache).

RadixAttention stores prefixes as a trie so multi-turn chains and partial prefix overlaps reuse shared sub-paths automatically; vLLM APC achieves the same via a flat hash table layered over the paged-block allocator.
Cached blocks are read-only and reference-counted: concurrent requests share the same physical blocks without locks, and a block becomes eligible for LRU eviction only when its ref-count drops to 0.

The payoff is geometric: skipping the P x P prefix corner of the causal attention triangle turns prefill cost from proportional to (P+S)^2 into proportional to S*P, a speedup of about (P+S)/S; across k agent turns this converts O(k^2 L) work into O(kL).

existing.name: prefixcache-block-hash-chain
existing.improvement: Renders cleanly and correctly in both themes. The chained-hash arrows (h0 feeds Block 1, etc.), the per-block HIT/MISS badges, and the cached-prefix vs uncached-suffix partition bar together nail intuition #2 with no clutter or clipping. No change needed.

existing.name: prefixcache-radix-tree
existing.issue: In BOTH themes the long red caption line ('Single trie lookup: O(P) longest-prefix match...') sits directly on top of the 'query tokens (session 1)/(session 2)' labels and grazes the branching arrows, producing an overlap smear that is especially muddy in dark mode. Separately, the figure only depicts a single fully-shared prefix that fans out to two sessions; it does not visually show the two features section 7.7.3 emphasizes as the trie's whole point: incremental multi-turn path growth and partial-overlap edge splitting.
existing.improvement: Add vertical breathing room so the O(P) caption occupies its own band, clear of the session labels and arrows (in dark theme give it a subtle backing so it never reads through the arrows). Then strengthen the trie metaphor: keep the shared system-prompt edge at the root, but make one branch a genuine radix split where two sessions share only the FIRST turn and then diverge (an intermediate split node), so the reader sees partial-overlap reuse, not just full-prefix reuse. Optionally annotate one edge label as multi-token ('A -> A+B -> A+B+C') to show incremental multi-turn growth. Preserve the 'KV cached / ref-counted' badges on the shared node.

existing.name: prefixcache-refcount-sharing
existing.issue: The timeline is information-dense and text collides with structure: annotation strings ('ref=2: A & B read same blocks', 'read-only - no locks needed', 'ref_count = 0 eligible for LRU eviction') sit directly on the dashed gridlines and on the step-plot line, and the top 'Block 0/1 (sys_prompt)' captions crowd their own borders. In dark theme these overlaps become hard to parse.
existing.improvement: The ref_count step plot is the star intuition (ref-count gates eviction) - give it more vertical height and let its 0->1->2->1->0 staircase dominate the lower half, with the value annotations placed in clear whitespace above or beside the steps, never on a gridline. Lift the annotation text off the dashed t1..t4 verticals. Loosen the top block labels so 'read-only / cached KV on GPU' text does not touch the block outline. Keep the copy-on-write summary band. Goal: same content, clearly layered reading order in both themes.

new_figures.name: prefixcache-attention-cost-geometry
new_figures.concept: Prefill compute drawn as a causal attention triangle: the cached prefix corner is skipped, only the thin suffix strip is computed, making the (P+S)/S speedup visually obvious.
new_figures.rationale: Section 7.7.7 carries the chapter's economic thesis with the cost model (P+S)^2/2 vs S*P + S^2/2 and speedup ~ (P+S)/S, but there is no figure for it. The intuition is fundamentally geometric and a single static frame makes it click far better than algebra: the reader SEES that the giant prefix-attends-to-prefix corner (already cached) is skipped and only the narrow bottom band of query rows must be computed. This is the 'why prefix caching is a first-order economic lever' picture the chapter is missing.
new_figures.placement: Section 7.7.7 The Economics of Shared Prefixes, right after the '### Cost model' subheading and its speedup formula.
new_figures.brief: Draw the causal (lower-triangular) attention score matrix for a sequence of length P+S. Rows = query positions (top to bottom), columns = key positions (left to right); only the lower triangle is populated because attention is causal. Use small illustrative integers so it fits: e.g. a prefix of ~7 cells wide and a suffix of ~2 cells, with a clearly marked boundary at column/row P. Partition the populated triangle into three labelled regions: (1) the large P x P lower-triangular CORNER at top-left = 'prefix attends to prefix -- KV already cached, NOT recomputed at request time'; render it hollow / hatched / de-emphasized to read as 'skipped / free'. (2) the S rows x P columns rectangle along the bottom-left = 'suffix attends to cached prefix -- reads cached K/V, no new K/V projections'; render as computed. (3) the small S x S lower triangle at bottom-right = 'suffix attends to suffix -- computed fresh'; render as computed. Make the visual weight tell the story: the skipped corner is large, the computed bottom band (regions 2+3) is a thin strip. Put the boundary line at position P with a tick labelled 'P (cached prefix)' on one side and 'S (new suffix)' on the other. Annotation block: 'Without cache: entire triangle, cost proportional to (P+S)^2 / 2.  With a warm prefix cache: only the bottom S rows are computed, cost proportional to S*P + S^2/2.  Speedup ~ (P+S)/S.' Theme-safe: one accent color only for the P boundary; use a neutral fill for 'computed' cells and hollow/hatched cells for the 'cached / skipped' corner so it works in light and dark; no hardcoded benchmark numbers, only the illustrative small integers and the symbolic P, S. Everything must fit inside the viewBox with no overlapping labels; the three region labels can sit in the margin with leader lines to each region.

new_figures.name: prefixcache-agent-turn-growth
new_figures.concept: Across k agent turns, cumulative prefill cost without caching grows as O(k^2 L) because every turn re-prefills the whole history, while a warm cache reuses the history and grows only as O(kL).
new_figures.rationale: The end of 7.7.7 makes a distinct and highly memorable claim for the agent audience -- prefix caching turns O(k^2 L) into O(kL), 'a qualitative improvement in long-horizon agent tasks' -- but this scaling intuition is separate from the single-request attention geometry and is unillustrated. A static side-by-side of per-turn work (re-prefill everything vs prefill only the new turn) makes the quadratic-triangle vs linear-strip contrast immediate, which is exactly the argument agent builders need.
new_figures.placement: Section 7.7.7, immediately after the paragraph ending 'so total cost scales as O(kL) -- a qualitative improvement in long-horizon agent tasks.'
new_figures.brief: Two-panel comparison over a small number of turns (e.g. turns 1..4), where each turn adds L new tokens (L a small illustrative integer / symbol). LEFT panel titled 'Without prefix caching': a vertical stack of horizontal bars, one per turn; bar for turn t has length t*L because the turn re-prefills the ENTIRE conversation so far (1L, 2L, 3L, 4L). Fill the whole bar as 'computed this turn'. The stack forms a staircase whose total area is a triangle -- annotate 'total prefill work grows as O(k^2 L)'. RIGHT panel titled 'With a warm prefix cache': same turns, but each bar is split into a long hollow 'reused from cache (no compute)' segment covering the prior history ((t-1)*L) and a single short filled segment of length L = 'only the new turn is prefilled'. All the filled segments are the same short length, so the computed area is a thin constant-width column -- annotate 'total prefill work grows as O(kL)'. Below both panels, a small companion plot: cumulative prefill work vs number of turns k, two curves -- an upward-bending quadratic (no cache) and a straight line (cache) -- with the growing gap shaded to drive home the qualitative difference. Label axes 'turn k' and 'cumulative prefill tokens'. Theme-safe: filled = computed, hollow/hatched = reused-from-cache, one accent for the cache curve; small illustrative integers only, no invented benchmark numbers; ensure bars, labels, and the companion plot all fit the viewBox without overlap.

summary: Chapter 7.7 is already well-served for its mechanism figures: block-hash-chain (keep, clean in both themes), radix-tree (refine -- caption overlaps session labels/arrows and the trie's partial-overlap and multi-turn growth aren't visually shown), and refcount-sharing (refine -- dense timeline where annotations sit on gridlines and the star ref_count step-plot needs room). The one genuinely under-served idea is the chapter's economic thesis (7.7.7): the geometric why. I propose a priority-1 static 'attention cost geometry' figure that shows the causal attention triangle with the cached P x P corner skipped and only the thin suffix strip computed, making speedup ~ (P+S)/S obvious; and a priority-2 static 'agent turn growth' figure contrasting O(k^2 L) re-prefill-everything vs O(kL) reuse across agent turns. All proposals are static -- no motion is essential -- and stay theme-safe with only illustrative small integers.
